heading: Special allocation rules for certain asset acquisitions
label: 1060 Special allocation rules for certain asset acquisitions
notes: Prior ProvisionsA prior section 1060 was renumbered .section 1063 of this title

Amendments1993—Subsec. (b)(1). , substituted “section 197 intangibles” for “goodwill or going concern value”.Pub. L. 103–66, § 13261(e)(1)Subsec. (d)(1). , substituted “section 197 intangibles” for “goodwill or going concern value (or similar items)”.Pub. L. 103–66, § 13261(e)(2)1990—Subsec. (a). , inserted at end “If in connection with an applicable asset acquisition, the transferee and transferor agree in writing as to the allocation of any consideration, or as to the fair market value of any of the assets, such agreement shall be binding on both the transferee and transferor unless the Secretary determines that such allocation (or fair market value) is not appropriate.”Pub. children.text: the transferee’s basis in such assets, and
the gain or loss of the transferor with respect to such acquisition,

children.heading: Information required to be furnished to Secretary
children.label: b Information required to be furnished to Secretary

children.text: The amount of the consideration received for the assets which is allocated to section 197 intangibles.
Any modification of the amount described in paragraph (1).
Any other information with respect to other assets transferred in such acquisition as the Secretary deems necessary to carry out the provisions of this section.
of assets which constitute a trade or business, and
with respect to which the transferee’s basis in such assets is determined wholly by reference to the consideration paid for such assets.

children.heading: Treatment of certain partnership transactions
children.label: d Treatment of certain partnership transactions

children.text: the rules of subsection (a) shall apply but only for purposes of determining the value of section 197 intangibles for purposes of applying section 755, and
if section 755 applies, such distribution or transfer (as the case may be) shall be treated as an applicable asset acquisition for purposes of subsection (b).

children.heading: Information required in case of certain transfers of interests in entities
children.label: e Information required in case of certain transfers of interests in entities
A
children.text: a person who is a 10-percent owner with respect to any entity transfers an interest in such entity, and

children.label: B
children.text: in connection with such transfer, such owner (or a related person) enters into an employment contract, covenant not to compete, royalty or lease agreement, or other agreement with the transferee,

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: The term “10-percent owner” means, with respect to any entity, any person who holds 10 percent or more (by value) of the interests in such entity immediately before the transfer.

children.heading: Constructive ownership
children.label: B Constructive ownership
children.text: Section 318 shall apply in determining ownership of stock in a corporation. Similar principles shall apply in determining the ownership of interests in any other entity.

children.heading: Related person
children.label: 3 Related person
children.text: For purposes of this subsection, the term “related person” means any person who is related (within the meaning of section 267(b) or 707(b)(1)) to the 10-percent owner.

children.heading: Cross reference
children.label: f Cross reference
children.text: For provisions relating to penalties for failure to file a return required by this section, see section 6721.
